description: Goal: expansion decides what top-level quoted source exists, while publication decides what scope-tier facts that source defines. Ordinary function-body expansion is no longer part of this layer. Signal: source sugar rewrites, require/import macro readiness, and item-level expansion live behind one explicit expander API; publication consumes already-expanded scope fragments and no longer mixes tree rewriting with scope mutation logic; ordinary function-body expansion happens on function demand, not during scope publication. Strategy: extract the item/scope expansion walk out of source_publish after the function-staging epic, reuse it for item-level expansion and surface fragments, preserve fact-graph blocking semantics, and leave publication with reserve/apply responsibilities only.
title: Compiler2 source expansion: split quoted root rewriting from fact publication
